{"query":"xz utils multithreaded decoder crash bug versions 5.3.3alpha 5.8.0 invalid input","model":"mako","response_time_ms":1626,"verdict":null,"extra_info":null,"results":[{"url":"https://tukaani.org/xz/threaded-decoder-early-free.html","title":"CVE-2025-31115: Threaded .xz decoder frees memory too early","content":"In XZ Utils 5.3.3alpha to 5.8.0, the multithreaded .xz decoder in liblzma has a bug where invalid input can at least result in a crash (CVE-2025-31115).
Applications and libraries that use the lzma_stream_decoder_mt function are affected.
The bug has been fixed in XZ Utils 5.8.1, and the fix has been committed to the v5.4, v5.6, v5.8, and master branches in the xz Git repository.
XZ Utils is fuzzed by OSS-Fuzz.
However, there was no program to fuzz the multithreaded .xz decoder.
Even if there had been, it likely would have used the fuzz_code function in fuzz_common.h like the existing fuzz targets did.
That function called lzma_code in such a way that it would have been impossible to trigger this particular bug.
A fuzzer has been added and fuzz_common.h has been modified."},{"url":"https://github.com/tukaani-project/xz/security/advisories/GHSA-6cc8-p5mm-29w2","title":"XZ Utils: Threaded decoder frees memory too early · Advisory · tukaani-project/xz · GitHub","content":"If two threads of execution use a resource simultaneously, there exists the possibility that resources may be used while invalid, in turn making the state of execution undefined.
The product reuses or references memory after it has been freed.
You can’t perform that action at this time."},{"url":"https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2025-31115","title":"NVD - CVE-2025-31115","content":"This CVE record is not being prioritized for NVD enrichment efforts due to resource or other concerns.
The effects include heap use after free and writing to an address based on the null pointer plus an offset.
No new release packages will be made from the old stable branches, but a standalone patch is available that applies to all affected releases.
CWE-416 — Use After Free — GitHub, Inc.

tavily.raw: {"query":"xz utils multithreaded decoder crash bug versions 5.3.3alpha 5.8.0 invalid input","follow_up_questions":null,"answer":null,"images":[],"results":[{"url":"https://feedly.com/cve/CVE-2025-31115","title":"CVE-2025-31115 - Exploits & Severity - Feedly","content":"# CVE-2025-31115. A vulnerability in XZ Utils' multithreaded .xz decoder (liblzma) affects versions 5.3.3alpha to 5.8.0. The bug involves invalid input potentially causing application crashes, with critical memory-related issues including heap use after free and writing to addresses based on a null pointer plus an offset. The vulnerability could allow attackers to exploit memory management weaknesses to destabilize or potentially compromise systems using affected XZ Utils versions. A fix has been implemented in XZ Utils version 5.8.1, with patches committed to v5.4, v5.6, v5.8, and master branches in the xz Git repository. NVD published the first details for CVE-2025-31115. A critical race condition in XZ Utils 5.3.3alpha-5.8.0 multithreaded .xz decoder (liblzma lzma\\_stream\\_decoder\\_mt) causes heap use-after-free and null pointer dereference, enabling remote denial of service and memory corruption on industrial automation platforms.","score":0.9501204,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.sentinelone.com/vulnerability-database/cve-2025-31115","title":"CVE-2025-31115: XZ Utils Use-After-Free Vulnerability","content":"Six years running.Find Out Why. Vulnerability Database/CVE-2025-31115. # CVE-2025-31115: XZ Utils Use-After-Free Vulnerability. CVE-2025-31115 is a use-after-free vulnerability in XZ Utils affecting the multithreaded .xz decoder in liblzma versions 5.3.3alpha to 5.8.0. ## CVE-2025-31115 Overview. In XZ Utils 5.3.3alpha to 5.8.0, the multithreaded .xz decoder in liblzma has a bug where invalid input can result in a crash. The bug is fixed in XZ Utils 5.8.1. * XZ Utils 5.3.3alpha. * XZ Utils 5.8.0. * 2025-04-03 - CVE-2025-31115 published to NVD. ## Technical Details for CVE-2025-31115. This vulnerability arises from improper handling of inputs in the lzma\\_stream\\_decoder\\_mt function, leading to potential heap use after free conditions. The root cause of this vulnerability is the lack of adequate validation in handling invalid inputs when decoding with the multithreaded .xz decoder. The attack vector for this vulnerability is network-based, requiring remote attackers to send crafted inputs to exploit the flaw. ## Detection Methods for CVE-2025-31115. ## How to Mitigate CVE-2025-31115.","score":0.9368016,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.linkedin.com/posts/cve-find_cve-2025-31115-high-xz-utils-versions-activity-7313614272364769281-eSUV","title":"CVE-2025-31115: XZ Utils bug in multithreaded decoder","content":"[CVE-2025-31115: HIGH] XZ Utils versions 5.3.3alpha to 5.8.0 have a bug in the multithreaded .xz decoder in liblzma, leading to crashes due to invalid input.","score":0.92715925,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://tukaani.org/xz/threaded-decoder-early-free.html","title":"CVE-2025-31115: Threaded .xz decoder frees memory too early","content":"tukaani.org » XZ Utils » CVE-2025-31115. # CVE-2025-31115: Threaded .xz decoder frees memory too early. In XZ Utils 5.3.3alpha to 5.8.0, the multithreaded .xz decoder in liblzma has a bug where invalid input can at least result in a crash (CVE-2025-31115). Applications and libraries that use the `lzma_stream_decoder_mt` function are affected. The bug has been fixed in XZ Utils 5.8.1, and the fix has been committed to the `v5.4`, `v5.6`, `v5.8`, and `master` branches in the xz Git repository. <xz-cve-2025-31115.patch> — signature. The single-threaded .xz decoder (`lzma_stream_decoder`) isn’t affected. The commands `xz --decompress --threads=1` and `xzdec` use the single-threaded decoder. However, there was no program to fuzz the multithreaded .xz decoder. Even if there had been, it likely would have used the `fuzz_code` function in `fuzz_common.h` like the existing fuzz targets did. That function called `lzma_code` in such a way that it would have been impossible to trigger this particular bug.","score":0.92491204,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://vulnerabilities.ncsc.nl/vulnerability.html?id=2025%2Fcve-2025-31115","title":"1 - NCSC | Kwetsbaarheden","content":"In XZ Utils 5.3.3alpha to 5.8.0, the multithreaded .xz decoder in liblzma has a bug where invalid input can at least result in a crash. The effects include","score":0.92442214,"raw_content":null}],"response_time":1.12,"request_id":"9dc50d05-ceef-48b8-b7ec-d2fa6719141d"}
